title: [podman] [logging] long `_CMDLINE` variable is filling the log
body:
## What did I do?
As normal user:
``` sh
podman run --name test1 --rm --log-driver=journald busybox sh -c "echo 1;"
```

As root:
``` sh
journalctl CONTAINER_NAME=test1 -o json 2>&1 | jq --sort-keys
```

## What did I get?
<details>
  <summary>Click to expand</summary>

  ``` json
{
  "CODE_FILE": "src/ctr_logging.c",
  "CODE_FUNC": "write_journald",
  "CODE_LINE": "264",
  "CONTAINER_ID": "b21c83a182fe",
  "CONTAINER_ID_FULL": "b21c83a182febc643825c87716e2b18590206dddac5c2287cb28995e0419bff2",
  "CONTAINER_NAME": "test1",
  "MESSAGE": "1\n",
  "PRIORITY": "6",
  "SYSLOG_IDENTIFIER": "conmon",
  "_AUDIT_LOGINUID": "0",
  "_AUDIT_SESSION": "208",
  "_BOOT_ID": "f3a5c44a5a7d49ecbb1e62c06e1f6394",
  "_CAP_EFFECTIVE": "1ffffffffff",
  "_CMDLINE": "/home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin//conmon --api-version 1 -c b21c83a182febc643825c87716e2b18590206dddac5c2287cb28995e0419bff2 -u b21c83a182febc643825c87716e2b18590206dddac5c2287cb28995e0419bff2 -r /home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin//crun -b /home/tobwen/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/b21c83a182febc643825c87716e2b18590206dddac5c2287cb28995e0419bff2/userdata -p /run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/b21c83a182febc643825c87716e2b18590206dddac5c2287cb28995e0419bff2/userdata/pidfile -n test1 --exit-dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/exits --full-attach -s -l journald --log-level warning --runtime-arg --log-format=json --runtime-arg --log --runtime-arg=/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/b21c83a182febc643825c87716e2b18590206dddac5c2287cb28995e0419bff2/userdata/oci-log --conmon-pidfile /run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/b21c83a182febc643825c87716e2b18590206dddac5c2287cb28995e0419bff2/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /home/tobwen/.local/share/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/containers --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg warning --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg systemd --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg /home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin//crun --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg --storage-opt --exit-command-arg overlay.mount_program=/home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin//fuse-overlayfs --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg journald --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg --rm --exit-command-arg b21c83a182febc643825c87716e2b18590206dddac5c2287cb28995e0419bff2",
  "_COMM": "conmon",
  "_EXE": "/home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin/conmon",
  "_GID": "1000",
  "_HOSTNAME": "debian",
  "_MACHINE_ID": "17c150fb4b8c4ba8ab5269a37510bcf9",
  "_PID": "16819",
  "_SELINUX_CONTEXT": "unconfined\n",
  "_SOURCE_REALTIME_TIMESTAMP": "1622943658552233",

  "_SYSTEMD_INVOCATION_ID": "135df6771a584735bf07954cee7e9cca",
  "_SYSTEMD_OWNER_UID": "1000",
  "_SYSTEMD_SLICE": "user-1000.slice",
  "_SYSTEMD_UNIT": "user@1000.service",
  "_SYSTEMD_USER_SLICE": "user.slice",
  "_SYSTEMD_USER_UNIT": "libpod-conmon-b21c83a182febc643825c87716e2b18590206dddac5c2287cb28995e0419bff2.scope",

  "_UID": "1000",
  "__CURSOR": "s=c1963d96335741caa540adadc36928f2;i=2c;b=f3a5c44a5a7d49ecbb1e62c06e1f6394;m=13fa276244;t=5c40f04422be6;x=eac09ce873fe2871",
  "__MONOTONIC_TIMESTAMP": "85801263684",
  "__REALTIME_TIMESTAMP": "1622943658552294"

  ```

## What is the issue?
`_CMDLINE` is pretty long and gets logged over and over again.

## What did I expect?
Would be nice to drop `_CMDLINE`, e.g. using `podman --log-opt`

## Which versions did I use?
- podman version 3.2.0-dev (https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/b6167cedb2ba69a66646a94eb27c49bbbea88e15)
- conmon version 2.0.30-dev (24c73c2abb3f220ebd97acf498b57c560ca26c04)

comments.body: this is more of an issue with podman. It is there that conmon's args are put together (specifically the args for the cleanup command, which contributes to the length quite a bit). moving there

I don't really know if we can fix this on the Podman side - we have to be explicit about all of this to make sure that the cleanup process gets launched with the exact config of the Podman that invoked Conmon - plus Conmon's arguments are pretty verbose to begin with. We could look into calling Conmon with configuration provided by file which would include all the very verbose flags we're passing via CLI right now, but that would require Conmon involvement to add support for config-from-file.
I'm asking myself if _journald_'s binary format de-duplicates this somehow. Let me figure it out.

## Update
There is no deduplication. The journal files are append-only, so it's not possible.
https://systemd.io/JOURNAL_FILE_FORMAT/
Okay, this only seems to happen at the start of a container... seems like my experiments were a little bit too hard :-) Closing, since this isn't a bug.
